impressum.title: Imprint
impressum.intro: Information pursuant to Section 5 of the German Telemedia Act (TMG).
impressum.responsibleHeading: Responsible for content pursuant to Section 18 (2) MStV
impressum.disputeBody: The European Commission provides a platform for online dispute resolution (ODR): https://ec.europa.eu/consumers/odr/. You can find our email address above in this imprint. We are neither willing nor obliged to participate in dispute resolution proceedings before a consumer arbitration board.
impressum.liabilityContentBody: As a service provider, we are responsible for our own content on these pages in accordance with Section 7 (1) TMG and general law. According to Sections 8 to 10 TMG, however, we as a service provider are not obliged to monitor transmitted or stored third-party information or to investigate circumstances that indicate illegal activity. Obligations to remove or block the use of information under general law remain unaffected.
impressum.liabilityLinksBody: Our offer contains links to external third-party websites over whose content we have no influence. We therefore cannot accept any liability for this third-party content. The respective provider or operator of the linked pages is always responsible for their content.
impressum.copyrightBody: The content and works created by the site operators on these pages are subject to German copyright law. Contributions by third parties are marked as such. The reproduction, processing, distribution and any kind of use beyond the limits of copyright law require the written consent of the respective author or creator.

datenschutz.title: Privacy Policy
datenschutz.intro: This privacy policy informs you about the nature, scope and purpose of the processing of personal data within our online offering, 2Fronts. The definitions of the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) apply.
datenschutz.controllerBody:
The controller within the meaning of the GDPR is:
Moad Kaoukab
Heerstr. 136, 47053 Duisburg
Email: moad@2fronts.de
datenschutz.scopeBody: 2Fronts is a B2B marketplace for ready-made automations. We process personal data when you create an account, purchase an automation, or connect a service (e.g. Google Sheets or Slack) to your automation. In detail, we use the data processors and interfaces described below.
datenschutz.legalBasisBody: We process personal data on the following legal bases: for the performance of a contract or pre-contractual measures (Art. 6 (1) (b) GDPR), to comply with legal obligations (Art. 6 (1) (c) GDPR), on the basis of your consent (Art. 6 (1) (a) GDPR), and to protect our legitimate interests, such as the secure and reliable operation of the service (Art. 6 (1) (f) GDPR).
datenschutz.supabaseBody: The frontend of this website is hosted externally by Vercel Inc. (440 N Barranca Ave #4133, Covina, CA 91723, USA). Technical access data (e.g. IP address, browser and device information, time of access) may be processed; transfer to the USA is based on the EU Standard Contractual Clauses. We host the application and our database with Supabase (Supabase, Inc.). On our behalf, Supabase processes account and inventory data (e.g. email address, encrypted password or OAuth identifier, timestamps) as well as the data you generate in the application (e.g. purchased automations, mappings, requests). The legal basis is Art. 6 (1) (b) and (f) GDPR. We have a data processing agreement with Supabase (Art. 28 GDPR). Where data is transferred to a third country, this is done on the basis of the EU Standard Contractual Clauses.
datenschutz.analyticsBody: We use Vercel Web Analytics, a service provided by Vercel Inc. (440 N Barranca Ave #4133, Covina, CA 91723, USA), to compile statistics about the use of our website (e.g. page views, country of origin, browser and device type). The service does not set cookies and does not store any data on your device. To distinguish visits, a hash value is derived from technical access data (including IP address and browser information); this hash changes daily, the IP address itself is not stored permanently, and visitors are not identifiable to us. URLs containing customer identifiers are anonymized before transmission. Processing may take place on servers in the USA; the transfer is based on the EU Standard Contractual Clauses. The legal basis is our legitimate interest in audience measurement and in improving our service (Art. 6 (1) (f) GDPR).
datenschutz.stripeBody: We process payments via Stripe (Stripe Payments Europe, Ltd. / Stripe, Inc.). When you purchase an automation, the data required for payment (e.g. name, email address, billing details and payment method information) is transmitted directly to Stripe and processed there. Complete payment data (e.g. credit card numbers) is processed exclusively by Stripe; we do not receive or store it. The legal basis is Art. 6 (1) (b) GDPR. Further information can be found in Stripe's privacy policy.
datenschutz.googleHeading: 6. Google OAuth and the Google Sheets API
datenschutz.googleBody: You can connect your Google account to 2Fronts via Google OAuth so that an automation can write to Google Sheets on your behalf. We access the Google Sheets API exclusively after your explicit authorization and only within the scope of the permissions (scopes) you have granted. In doing so, we process your Google account identifier, OAuth access and refresh tokens, and the contents of the spreadsheets you select, insofar as this is necessary to run the automation. You can revoke access at any time in your Google account settings at https://myaccount.google.com/permissions. The legal basis is your consent (Art. 6 (1) (a) GDPR) and Art. 6 (1) (b) GDPR.
datenschutz.googleLimitedUseHeading: 6.1 Google API Services User Data Policy — Limited Use
datenschutz.googleLimitedUseBody: 2Fronts' use and transfer of information received from Google APIs to any other app adheres to the Google API Services User Data Policy, including the Limited Use requirements.
datenschutz.googleLimitedUseVerbatimLabel: Verbatim wording (Google API Services User Data Policy):
datenschutz.googleLimitedUseVerbatim: 2Fronts' use and transfer to any other app of information received from Google APIs will adhere to the Google API Services User Data Policy, including the Limited Use requirements.
datenschutz.slackBody: You can connect a Slack workspace to 2Fronts so that an automation can post notifications (e.g. new leads) to a channel you select. Access takes place exclusively after your authorization via Slack OAuth and only within the scope of the permissions granted. In doing so, we process the workspace and channel identifier, the access token issued by Slack, and the contents of the messages sent by the automation. You can revoke access at any time in your Slack workspace settings. The legal basis is your consent (Art. 6 (1) (a) GDPR) and Art. 6 (1) (b) GDPR.
datenschutz.cookiesBody: 2Fronts does not use tracking or advertising cookies. We use your browser's local storage (localStorage) exclusively for technically necessary purposes, in particular to store your language choice (DE/EN) and to manage your login session. This data remains in your browser and is not transmitted to us for analytics purposes. The legal basis is Art. 6 (1) (f) GDPR and Section 25 (2) TTDSG (technically necessary storage).
datenschutz.retentionBody: We store personal data only for as long as is necessary for the respective purposes or as required by statutory retention periods (in particular under commercial and tax law). Once the purpose no longer applies, the data is deleted or anonymized.
datenschutz.rightsHeading: 10. Your rights as a data subject
datenschutz.rightsBody: Under the GDPR you have the following rights: the right of access (Art. 15), to rectification (Art. 16), to erasure (Art. 17), to restriction of processing (Art. 18), to data portability (Art. 20), and to object to processing (Art. 21). You may withdraw any consent given at any time with effect for the future (Art. 7 (3)). To exercise your rights, please contact moad@2fronts.de.
datenschutz.complaintHeading: 11. Right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority
datenschutz.complaintBody: Without prejudice to any other administrative or judicial remedy, you have the right to lodge a complaint with a data protection supervisory authority, in particular in the Member State of your residence, place of work or place of the alleged infringement (Art. 77 GDPR).
datenschutz.aiEmailHeading: 12. AI Concierge and email delivery (Google, Resend)
datenschutz.aiEmailBody: Anyone using a customer's AI Booking Concierge (the public chat at /c/...) enters their name, email address and chat messages. To generate replies, this content is processed by Google (Gemini API, Google Ireland Ltd. / Google LLC); this may involve a transfer to the USA on the basis of the EU Standard Contractual Clauses. Name, email address and the conversation are also stored for the respective coach so they can contact the prospect. If you send a suggestion via the "Didn't find what you're looking for?" form, we process your email address and, optionally, your industry and message. To send system and notification emails (e.g. request notifications, confirmation emails) we use the provider Resend (Resend, Inc., USA); the recipient's email address is processed and the transfer is based on the EU Standard Contractual Clauses. The legal basis is Art. 6(1)(b) and (f) GDPR and, where you have given consent, Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR.
datenschutz.changesBody: We reserve the right to amend this privacy policy so that it always complies with current legal requirements or in order to implement changes to our services. The current version applies to your next visit.

agb.title: General Terms and Conditions (GTC)
agb.intro: These General Terms and Conditions apply to the use of the 2Fronts marketplace and the purchase of the automations and subscriptions offered there — to entrepreneurs (Section 14 BGB) as well as to consumers (Section 13 BGB). Consumers have a statutory right of withdrawal; details are set out in the Withdrawal Policy.
agb.scopeBody: These GTC apply to all contracts concerning the use of the 2Fronts marketplace (the "Provider", Moad Kaoukab, Heerstr. 136, 47053 Duisburg) and the purchase of the automations offered there. Conflicting terms of the customer, or terms deviating from these GTC, are not recognized unless the Provider expressly agrees to their validity in writing.
agb.serviceBody: 2Fronts provides ready-made automations that can be set up after purchase and connected to the customer's third-party services (e.g. Google Sheets or Slack). The specific scope of services results from the respective product description in the marketplace at the time of purchase. The Provider is not responsible for the availability, functionality or conditions of the connected third-party services.
agb.contractBody: The presentation of automations in the marketplace does not constitute a binding offer but an invitation to submit an offer. By completing the purchase process, the customer submits a binding offer. The contract is concluded upon confirmation of the purchase or provision of the automation by the Provider.
agb.pricesBody: The prices stated in the marketplace at the time of purchase apply; these are final prices. As a small business under Section 19 of the German VAT Act (Kleinunternehmer §19 UStG), the provider does not charge VAT, so none is shown separately. Payment is processed via our payment service provider Stripe; its payment-processing terms apply in addition. Subscriptions taken out for the first time start with a free 14-day trial period; a valid payment method is stored at the time of ordering. No charge is made during the trial period; the first charge takes place after the trial period ends. Thereafter, the amount is charged recurrently at the chosen billing interval (e.g. monthly) to the stored payment method until the subscription is cancelled. If a customer takes out a new subscription after a previous one, no further trial period is granted; in that case the first charge takes place upon conclusion of the contract. The customer ensures that the payment data they provide is accurate.
agb.obligationsBody: The customer is obliged to keep their access credentials confidential and to correctly grant the permissions required to set up the automation (e.g. OAuth authorizations for Google or Slack). The customer is solely responsible for ensuring that they are entitled to grant these permissions and that the processed data may be used lawfully.
agb.availabilityBody: The Provider endeavours to keep the service available as uninterrupted as possible but does not owe any specific availability. Maintenance work, disruptions at third-party services, and circumstances beyond the Provider's control (force majeure) may lead to temporary restrictions.
agb.liabilityBody: The Provider is liable without limitation for damages resulting from injury to life, body or health and for damages based on intent or gross negligence. In the case of simple negligence, the Provider is liable only for the breach of essential contractual obligations (cardinal obligations) and limited in amount to the foreseeable damage typical for the contract. Any further liability is excluded. Liability under the German Product Liability Act remains unaffected.
agb.ipBody: Upon full payment, the customer receives a simple, non-transferable right to use the purchased automation for their own business purposes. All intellectual property rights in the automations and the platform remain with the Provider.
agb.termBody: One-time purchases are completed upon delivery of the automation. Subscriptions run for an indefinite period at the chosen billing interval (e.g. monthly) and can be cancelled by the customer at any time, effective at the end of the current billing period — conveniently in the account under "My requests" via the "Manage / cancel subscription" button (Stripe customer portal). After cancellation, access remains available until the end of the current billing period or trial period and ends when that period expires. If the subscription is cancelled during the trial period, no charges are incurred. The right to extraordinary termination for good cause remains unaffected.
agb.lawHeading: 10. Applicable law and place of jurisdiction
agb.lawBody: The law of the Federal Republic of Germany applies, excluding the UN Convention on Contracts for the International Sale of Goods (CISG). The exclusive place of jurisdiction for all disputes arising from or in connection with this contract is the Provider's registered office, provided the customer is a merchant, a legal entity under public law, or a special fund under public law.
agb.severabilityBody: Should individual provisions of these GTC be or become invalid, the validity of the remaining provisions remains unaffected. The statutory provision shall apply in place of the invalid provision.

widerruf.title: Right of Withdrawal
widerruf.intro: The following withdrawal policy applies to consumers within the meaning of Section 13 BGB who enter into a contract via 2Fronts.
widerruf.rightBody: You have the right to withdraw from this contract within fourteen days without giving any reason. The withdrawal period is fourteen days from the day the contract was concluded. To exercise your right of withdrawal, you must inform us (Moad Kaoukab, Heerstr. 136, 47053 Duisburg, email: moad@2fronts.de) of your decision to withdraw from this contract by an unequivocal statement (e.g. a letter sent by post or an email). You may use the model withdrawal form below, but it is not mandatory. To meet the withdrawal deadline, it is sufficient that you send your communication concerning the exercise of the right of withdrawal before the withdrawal period has expired.
widerruf.consequencesBody: If you withdraw from this contract, we shall reimburse all payments received from you without undue delay and at the latest within fourteen days from the day on which we receive notification of your withdrawal. We will use the same means of payment that you used for the original transaction, unless expressly agreed otherwise with you; in no event will you be charged any fees for this reimbursement.
widerruf.digitalHeading: Early expiry of the right of withdrawal
widerruf.digitalBody: For contracts for the provision of services, your right of withdrawal expires if we have fully performed the service and only began performance after you gave your express consent and at the same time acknowledged that you would lose your right of withdrawal upon full performance of the contract. For the supply of digital content not delivered on a physical medium, the right of withdrawal expires accordingly if performance began with your express consent and you acknowledged that you thereby lose your right of withdrawal.
widerruf.formBody:
(If you wish to withdraw from the contract, please complete and return this form.)

To: Moad Kaoukab, Heerstr. 136, 47053 Duisburg, email: moad@2fronts.de

I/we (*) hereby withdraw from the contract concluded by me/us (*) for the purchase of the following service/automation:
____________________________________________

Ordered on (*) / received on (*): ____________________

Address of consumer(s): ____________________
Date: ____________________

(*) Delete as appropriate.

appHome.title: 2Fronts
appHome.heroSub: From now on, your AI answers your audience's inquiries — in seconds, around the clock, strictly from your content.
appHome.whatBody: Prospects chat on your own page with an AI that answers strictly from your content. It handles their questions, asks your qualification questions, and sends ready-to-book visitors straight to your calendar. You see every conversation in your dashboard.
appHome.feature1Body: You add your offer, prices, and common questions. Your setter makes nothing up — what it doesn't know, it won't promise.
appHome.feature2Body: Add your content, share your link, done. No onboarding calls, no implementation fees.
appHome.feature3Body: Every conversation in your dashboard, cancel any time. Your data stays yours.
appHome.privacyLine: We handle your data in line with our
appHome.signInPrompt: Already a customer or invited to test?
appHome.footerNote: 2Fronts is operated for business customers. Sales are B2B.

conciergeSetup.intro: Your offer, your prices, your answers. Your setter advises from this and nothing else — what you don't add, it won't promise.

welcome.title: Let's set up your setter.
welcome.body: Five quick questions — then your setter answers your audience and books sales calls around the clock. Takes about a minute.
welcome.languageLabel: Which language should your setter speak?

business.title: What's your business called?
business.hint: This is how your setter introduces itself.

offer.title: What do you offer?
offer.hint: One short paragraph: what it is, who it's for, the outcome. Your setter answers only from this.
offer.placeholder: We help early-stage founders land their first 10 customers through weekly 1:1 coaching…
offer.scrapePrompt: In a hurry? Paste your website and we'll draft this for you.
offer.scrapeDone: Here's a first draft. Tweak anything that's off.
offer.scrapeFailed: We couldn't read that page, no worries. Just type it in below.

questions.title: What do people usually ask before booking?
questions.hint: Paste the questions and objections you hear most, with your answers. Optional, you can add these later.
questions.placeholder:
How much does it cost? — Plans start at €200/month.
How long are sessions? — 45 minutes, weekly.

booking.title: Paste your booking link.
booking.label: Booking link
booking.hint: Calendly, Cal.com, or any scheduling link. This is where your setter sends everyone ready to book.

qualify.sub: Pick criteria your setter uses to sort visitors in chat, with clear answer buttons. Optional, you can skip it.
qualify.qualifiesHint: Checked = this answer counts as qualified

tone.title: How should it sound?
tone.hint: Pick the voice that fits your brand.

done.title: You're live.

conciergeEmbed.title: Add it to your website
conciergeEmbed.intro: One line of code is all it takes — your setter then appears as a chat bubble in the bottom-right corner of your site.
conciergeEmbed.step2: Paste it into your website — see below for how to do it in your site builder.
conciergeEmbed.step3: Save and reload your site. The chat bubble appears in the bottom-right corner.
conciergeEmbed.platformsLabel: How to do it in your website builder:

wordpress.name: WordPress
wordpress.hint: Open the Customizer and paste the line under "Additional JavaScript". Or use a plugin like "Header/Footer Code".

wix.name: Wix
wix.hint: Go to Settings → Custom Code and add the line for all pages.

squarespace.name: Squarespace
squarespace.hint: Go to Settings → Advanced → Code Injection and paste the line there.

webflow.name: Webflow
webflow.hint: Open Project Settings → Custom Code and paste the line into the footer field.

jimdo.name: Jimdo
jimdo.hint: Edit your site's head section and paste the line there.

demo.title: See what you get
demo.sub: Five minutes of the real product: how the setter is set up, how it qualifies, and how a call lands in your calendar.
demo.posterProspect: Hi! Is there still a spot in your program?
demo.posterSetter: Happy to help! Two quick questions, then you can pick a time.

nightStory.title: She wrote at 11:12pm. By 8:34am she was gone.
nightStory.sub: This is how coaches lose clients every week: the inquiry comes at night, the reply comes in the morning, the booking goes elsewhere.
nightStory.resolve: The 2Fronts setter answers in seconds, qualifies, and books the call. Even at 11:12pm.

calendarPain.title: And the open booking link? It fills your calendar. With the wrong people.
calendarPain.sub: Put your booking link in your bio and you get the opposite problem: anyone can book. So everyone does. People who want free tips, people with no budget, people who never show up.
calendarPain.resolve: The 2Fronts setter asks first: what is it about, how serious is it, does the budget fit. Nobody sees your booking link before answering those questions, and every answer shows up tagged in your dashboard. At night it does the asking, in the morning you know exactly who's on your calendar and how serious they are.

silentPain.kicker: EVERY WEEK · THE ONES YOU NEVER SAW
silentPain.title: You'll never know how many you lost.
silentPain.sub: Most people look at your offer and leave again. Without a word. You never even notice they were there. Taking that first step and actually writing to you, the coach, is something only a few ever manage.
silentPain.resolve: The setter asks first, before anyone has to work up the nerve to write. So the ones who would have quietly left already have a reason to answer instead.

offer.bullet1: Answers at 11:12pm, only from your content
offer.bullet2: Asks your qualifying questions and filters out tire-kickers
offer.bullet3: Books discovery calls straight into your calendar
offer.note: cancel monthly · secure checkout via Stripe

nightFinale.kicker: EVERY WEEK · NOW YOU SEE THEM
nightFinale.title: Next week, you'll know.
nightFinale.sub: Your setter starts the conversation before the tab closes, logs every visitor who answers, and books the call. Nothing happens quietly anymore.
